title: A tradition worth keeping in a small town
summary: Describe a specific local tradition and defend its value in modern life, acknowledging those who might see it as outdated.
topic: A tradition worth keeping in a small town
prompt: Write 3 paragraphs (350–500 words) describing a tradition practised in a small town or close community, and arguing that it is worth maintaining. Include a concession with 'although' or 'even if', a conditional sentence, the present perfect for continuity, and at least one relative clause.
hintStarters: Every spring in the town of Hallstatt, Austria — and in dozens of places like it — people gather in the main square to…
Although some residents see it as old-fashioned, I would argue that the tradition of…
If this tradition disappeared, the town would lose not just a custom but…

hintVocab.meaningVi: giữ gìn / tuân theo một truyền thống
trở nên lỗi thời, không còn phổ biến
cảm giác liên tục, gắn kết với quá khứ
một truyền thống còn sống động
tồn tại lâu hơn, vượt qua

sampleText:
Every September, the residents of a small town called Collio, in the Italian Alps, carry handmade lanterns through the streets after dark. The walk takes about forty minutes, and it ends in the main square, where the lanterns are set down and people eat together. The tradition has been practised for over two hundred years. Nobody remembers exactly how it started, but that does not seem to matter — what matters is that it has continued, generation after generation, even when the region's younger residents left to work in Milan or Turin and came back specifically to take part.

Although some people argue that such events are merely nostalgic — a way of preserving a past that the world has already moved past — I think this misses what a local tradition actually does. It is not about refusing to change. It is about creating a fixed point in the year that belongs entirely to that place and those people. The families who take part in the lantern walk are not the same ones who walked it a century ago, but they carry something forward that those earlier families began, and that continuity gives the town a shared identity which no amount of development can easily replace. If this tradition were to fade away, Collio would not only lose a ceremony — it would lose one of the few things that makes it distinctly itself rather than simply another small town in the mountains.

What I have observed about traditions that survive is that they are never truly static. The lanterns in Collio have changed shape over the years. Some years the route has been shortened; other years a local school has added music. The tradition has survived not because it has been preserved in a glass case but because each generation has renewed it in a small way, keeping the core while adjusting the details. Even if a tradition needs to change slightly to stay alive, that is a sign of health, not compromise. A tradition that adapts is one that still means something to the people who practise it — and that is the only kind worth keeping.

chỉ là hoài niệm đơn thuần

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Present perfect for ongoing continuity
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'The tradition has been practised for over two hundred years' và 'What I have observed about traditions that survive…' dùng thì hiện tại hoàn thành để nhấn mạnh rằng một điều gì đó bắt đầu trong quá khứ và vẫn còn liên quan hoặc tiếp diễn đến tận ngày nay — dùng 'for' để chỉ khoảng thời gian.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'The tradition has been practised for over two hundred years' and 'What I have observed about traditions that survive…' both use the present perfect to emphasise that something started in the past and remains relevant or ongoing today — 'for' is used to indicate a duration of time.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Even if for a concession about conditions
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'Even if a tradition needs to change slightly to stay alive, that is a sign of health, not compromise' dùng 'even if' để nhượng bộ một điều kiện có thể xảy ra — nghĩa là 'dù cho điều đó có xảy ra đi nữa, kết quả vẫn như vậy'. Khác với 'even though', 'even if' mang tính giả định hơn.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'Even if a tradition needs to change slightly to stay alive, that is a sign of health, not compromise' uses 'even if' to concede a possible condition — meaning 'regardless of whether that happens, the conclusion still holds'. Unlike 'even though', 'even if' is more hypothetical.

sampleTranslationVi:
Mỗi tháng Chín, cư dân của một thị trấn nhỏ tên Collio, ở dãy Alps của Ý, mang đèn lồng thủ công đi qua các con phố sau khi trời tối. Cuộc đi bộ mất khoảng bốn mươi phút, và kết thúc ở quảng trường chính, nơi đèn lồng được đặt xuống và mọi người ăn cùng nhau. Truyền thống này đã được thực hành hơn hai trăm năm. Không ai nhớ chính xác nó bắt đầu như thế nào, nhưng điều đó dường như không quan trọng — điều quan trọng là nó đã tiếp tục, thế hệ này qua thế hệ khác, ngay cả khi những cư dân trẻ của vùng rời đi làm việc ở Milan hay Turin và quay về đặc biệt để tham gia.

Mặc dù một số người cho rằng các sự kiện như vậy chỉ mang tính hoài niệm — một cách gìn giữ quá khứ mà thế giới đã vượt qua — tôi nghĩ điều này bỏ qua điều mà một truyền thống địa phương thực sự làm. Đó không phải là về việc từ chối thay đổi. Đó là về việc tạo ra một điểm cố định trong năm thuộc hoàn toàn về nơi đó và những người đó. Những gia đình tham gia cuộc đi bộ đèn lồng không phải là những gia đình đã đi nó một thế kỷ trước, nhưng họ mang điều gì đó tiếp nối mà những gia đình trước đó đã bắt đầu, và sự liên tục đó mang lại cho thị trấn một bản sắc chung mà không có sự phát triển nào có thể dễ dàng thay thế. Nếu truyền thống này dần phai nhạt, Collio sẽ không chỉ mất một nghi lễ — mà sẽ mất một trong số ít điều khiến nó mang bản sắc riêng biệt thay vì chỉ là một thị trấn nhỏ khác trong núi.

Điều tôi đã quan sát về các truyền thống tồn tại lâu dài là chúng không bao giờ thực sự tĩnh tại. Đèn lồng ở Collio đã thay đổi hình dạng qua nhiều năm. Một số năm tuyến đường đã được rút ngắn; những năm khác, một trường học địa phương đã thêm nhạc. Truyền thống đã tồn tại không phải vì nó được giữ trong tủ kính mà vì mỗi thế hệ đã đổi mới nó theo một cách nhỏ, giữ cốt lõi trong khi điều chỉnh các chi tiết. Dù cho một truyền thống cần thay đổi một chút để tiếp tục sống, đó là dấu hiệu của sự sống động, không phải sự thỏa hiệp. Một truyền thống thích nghi là một truyền thống vẫn còn ý nghĩa đối với những người thực hành nó — và đó là loại duy nhất đáng được giữ gìn.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses the present perfect passive correctly?
options: The tradition has been practised for over two hundred years.
The tradition has been practiced since over two hundred years.
The tradition has practised for over two hundred years.
The tradition have been practised for over two hundred years.
mcQuestions.explanation: Present perfect passive = 'has/have + been + past participle'; 'for' is used with a duration, not 'since'.
mcQuestions.hint: Passive present perfect: 'has been + past participle'; use 'for' with a period of time.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'even if' correctly to introduce a hypothetical concession?
options: Even if a tradition needs to change slightly but that is a sign of health.
Even if a tradition needs to change slightly, that is a sign of health.
Even if, a tradition needs to change slightly, that is a sign of health.
Even if a tradition would need to change slightly, that is a sign of health.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Even if + present simple, main clause' — no extra 'but', no comma after 'even if', and present simple (not 'would') in the if-clause.
mcQuestions.hint: 'Even if' works like 'if' — no 'but' in the main clause, and use present simple in the condition.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses a conditional correctly to describe a future loss?
options: If this tradition was to fade away, Collio will lose one of the few things that makes it distinctly itself.
If this tradition would fade away, Collio would lose one of the few things that makes it distinctly itself.
If this tradition were to fade away, Collio would lose one of the few things that makes it distinctly itself.
If this tradition fades away, Collio would lose one of the few things that makes it distinctly itself.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Were to + base verb' in the if-clause is a formal way to express a hypothetical future; it pairs with 'would' in the main clause.
mcQuestions.hint: 'If … were to + verb, … would + verb' is a formal conditional for unlikely or hypothetical futures.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'although' correctly to introduce a counterargument?
options: Although some people argue that such events are merely nostalgic but I think this misses the point.
Although some people argue that such events are merely nostalgic, however I think this misses the point.
Some people argue although such events are merely nostalgic, I think this misses the point.
Although some people argue that such events are merely nostalgic, I think this misses the point.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Although + clause, main clause' — no extra 'but' or 'however' in the main clause.
mcQuestions.hint: Use 'although' alone — don't add 'but' or 'however' as well; that creates a double connector.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses a relative clause correctly to describe a noun?
options: One of the few things that makes it distinctly itself rather than simply another small town.
One of the few things which it makes it distinctly itself rather than simply another small town.
One of the few things that make it distinctly itself who rather than simply another small town.
One of the few things that it makes it distinctly itself rather than simply another small town.
mcQuestions.explanation: After 'one of the few things', use 'that' (or 'which') without repeating the subject pronoun 'it'.
mcQuestions.hint: In a relative clause, 'that' is the subject — don't add 'it' as a second subject.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which phrase uses 'pass down' correctly in context?
options: The ceremony has been passed down from several generations.
The ceremony has been passed down through several generations.
The ceremony has been passed through several generations down.
The ceremony has passed down through several generations.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Pass down through generations' is the natural collocation; use the passive 'has been passed down' when the agent is unspecified.
mcQuestions.hint: 'Pass down' is a phrasal verb; in passive, use 'has been passed down through' — not 'from'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses 'no amount of' to express impossibility?
options: No amount of development is able to easy replace that shared identity.
No amounts of development can easily replace that shared identity.
No amount of development can easily replace that shared identity.
Not any amount of development can easily replacing that shared identity.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'No amount of + uncountable noun + can/could' is a fixed emphatic structure; 'amount' does not take a plural 's'.
mcQuestions.hint: 'No amount of' is always followed by an uncountable noun — no plural 's' on 'amount'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses 'keep + gerund' to describe an ongoing action?
options: Each generation has kept to renew the tradition in small ways.
Each generation has kept renew the tradition in small ways.
Each generation has keeped renewing the tradition in small ways.
Each generation has kept renewing the tradition in small ways.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Keep + gerund' = continue doing something; 'kept' is the past participle of 'keep', not 'keeped'.
mcQuestions.hint: 'Keep' is irregular: past and past participle are both 'kept'. 'Keep' takes a gerund, not 'to + verb'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'not just A but B' correctly to add a second idea?
options: Collio would lose not just a ceremony but one of the things that makes it distinctly itself.
Collio would lose not just a ceremony but also however one of the things that makes it distinctly itself.
Collio would not just lose a ceremony but only one of the things that makes it distinctly itself.
Collio would lose not only just a ceremony but one of the things that makes it distinctly itself.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Not just A but B' cleanly adds a second, stronger point; 'also however' is redundant and 'not only just' is incorrect.
mcQuestions.hint: 'Not just A but B' — keep it clean; don't double up with 'also' or 'however'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'come back to' correctly to describe returning for a purpose?
options: Younger residents left to work in Milan and came back specific to take part.
Younger residents left to work in Milan and came back specifically to take part.
Younger residents left to work in Milan and came back specifically for taking part.
Younger residents left to work in Milan and came back specifically to taking part.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Specifically to + base verb' expresses purpose; 'for + gerund' is also correct but 'specifically for taking' is less common here, and 'to taking' is wrong.
mcQuestions.hint: Use 'to + base verb' to express purpose after 'came back'; 'to taking' is never correct.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses 'what I have observed' as a noun clause?
options: What I observed about traditions that survive are that they are never truly static.
What have I observed about traditions that survive is that they are never truly static.
What I have observed about traditions that survive is that they are never truly static.
What I have observed about traditions that survive are they never truly static.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'What I have observed… is that…' — the noun clause 'what I have observed' takes a singular verb 'is'; inversion is not used in embedded questions.
mcQuestions.hint: 'What I have observed' is a noun clause (subject), so use 'is' — no inversion, no plural 'are'.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Is there a difference between a tradition that a community chooses to continue and one that outsiders — tourists, governments, or heritage organisations — pressure them to maintain? Does external attention help or change such traditions?
